content.full_text:
April 3, 1991

VIA TELECOPIER AND U.S. MAIL

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J. Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804

Re: Rita Walters

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

This letter is intended to confirm my conversation of yesterday with John Wallace of your office. As I advised Mr. Wallace, Ms. Rita Walters would like to use funds raised in connection with her candidacy for the Los Angeles Board of Education for her candidacy for Los Angeles City Council (the election for which occurs on April 9, 1991). All of these funds were raised with respect to one event which occurred in late 1989, and all were deposited in the State Fair Political Practices Commission account I.D. No. 891804, which is in the name of "Committee to Re-elect Rita Walters". This account was created in late 1989 after the passage of Proposition 73. The only sums ever deposited into this account were raised in connection with the single event referred to above.

Ms. Walters has examined contributions deposited into account No. 891904 to determine whether, when aggregated with contributions to Ms. Walters' campaign for City Council, any contributor would have donated in excess of $500. In accordance with that examination, Ms. Walters has refunded from account No. 891904 such sums as are necessary so that, if all funds in this account are transferred to the City Council campaign, no donor will have contributed more than $500 to that campaign.

Mr. Wallace has advised me that, in these circumstances, there is no state law ban on transfers between Ms. Walters' accounts; accordingly, in these circumstances, the transfer of funds from Ms. Walters' Board of Education campaign account to her City Council campaign account is lawful under state law.

Very truly yours,

Gregory R. Smith

GRS:as

cc: Anthony Alperin (by telecopier)
     Rita Walters (by telecopier)
